commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=881a33b3270627e9d15a68ab0e084eb8fa488c29)
commands.review_summary:
The branch adds deferred retained-lock idle callbacks so embedded attempt session locks release after abort, prompt release, or dispose is invoked from inside an active retained write.

PR surface: Source +17. Total +17 across 1 file.

Reproducibility: no. live high-confidence reproduction was run in this review. Source inspection shows the current-main false branch returns without releasing while session-write-lock reclaim skips locks still held by this process, which matches the PR's reported orphan-lock timeout path.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Human review is needed because the external PR must supply real behavior proof and the remaining blocker is validation of a concurrency-sensitive session-lock path, not an automation-safe code repair alone.

target.title: fix: force-release session lock on dispose to prevent orphan locks
target.body:
## Problem

When an embedded attempt (sub-agent) is torn down via `dispose()`, the
`disposeHeldLockAfterRetainedIdle()` function silently returns without
releasing the session write lock if a write operation is still in progress
(`retainedLockUseCount > 0` with `activeWriteLock` store active).

The same silent-return issue exists in `releaseHeldLockWithFence()`, used
by `releaseHeldLockForAbort()`.

This leaves orphaned `.jsonl.lock` files on disk that block subsequent
sub-agents from writing to the same session file. Because the lock's PID
matches the still-alive gateway process and the in-memory `state.held`
map still tracks the entry, the contention handler does not reclaim it
(`heldByThisProcess=true` → `shouldTreatAsOrphanSelfLock` returns false).
The lock is neither reclaimed nor released until the watchdog force-releases
it after `maxHoldMs` (default 5 min), but the watchdog only checks in-memory
entries and the file lock can persist indefinitely in some scenarios.

Result: new sub-agents get a 60-second timeout with "session file locked"
errors.

## Fix

In both `disposeHeldLockAfterRetainedIdle()` and
`releaseHeldLockWithFence()`, when `waitForRetainedLockIdle()` returns
`false`, force-release the lock (`heldLock = undefined; lock.release()`)
instead of silently returning. Teardown and abort paths must never leak a
lock.

## Files changed

`src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.session-lock.ts` — two
functions modified, 16 lines added.
target.author: longkunbetter

commands.author: anagnorisis2peripeteia

target.title: feat(local-container): native checkpoint fork via docker run (follow-up to #206)
target.body:
## Summary

Follow-up to #206. That PR lands docker-commit checkpoint **create / verify / delete** for the local-container provider but intentionally leaves **fork** unimplemented (ClawSweeper "option 1"). This PR adds the fork so `crabbox checkpoint fork <checkpoint>` launches a new local-container lease from the committed image instead of erroring on a missing provider hook.

> **Stacked on #206** — please review/merge #206 first. Until it lands, this PR's diff also shows the #206 commit; it reduces to the fork-only change afterward.

## How it works

`checkpoint fork` already routes native checkpoint records through `NativeCheckpointForkProvider.ApplyNativeCheckpointForkConfig`. The local-container provider now implements it: it points `cfg.LocalContainer.Image` at the checkpoint's committed image (`Record.Resource`, falling back to `Record.ImageID`), and the existing local-container backend builds the new lease from `LocalContainer.Image`. Verify messaging for docker-commit checkpoints is restored from `delete_local` to `fork_or_delete_local` now that fork works.

This mirrors the other native providers' fork hooks (`aws` sets `cfg.AWSAMI`, `gcp` sets `cfg.GCPMachineImage`, etc.).

## Files changed

- `internal/providers/localcontainer/provider.go` — `ApplyNativeCheckpointForkConfig`
- `internal/cli/checkpoint.go` — docker-commit verify `next_action`: `delete_local` -> `fork_or_delete_local`
- `internal/providers/localcontainer/checkpoint_test.go` — unit tests (sets image, prefers Resource, rejects other kinds)
- `internal/cli/checkpoint_dockercommit_proof_test.go` — `dockerproof` integration test now boots a container from the committed image (the fork) before deleting

## Real Docker proof — create -> verify -> fork -> delete

```
$ go test -tags dockerproof -run TestDockerCommitCheckpointLifecycleProof -v ./internal/cli/
=== RUN   TestDockerCommitCheckpointLifecycleProof
    source container: c52031734d5b
    CREATE: image=crabbox-checkpoint-proof id=62de16c36ffd state=available kind=docker-commit direct=true
    VERIFY: image present (next_action -> fork_or_delete_local), id=62de16c36ffd
    FORK:   booted container from checkpoint image -> forked-from-checkpoint
    DELETE: Untagged: crabbox-checkpoint-proof:latest / Deleted sha256:62de16c36ffd...
    CONFIRMED: crabbox-checkpoint-proof removed
--- PASS: TestDockerCommitCheckpointLifecycleProof (1.82s)
ok      github.com/openclaw/crabbox/internal/cli
```

Built/tested in `golang:1.26` + static docker client against host Docker `29.0.1` over a mounted socket (Go is unavailable on the dev machine). `go build ./...`, `go vet`, and the checkpoint unit tests for both `internal/cli` and `internal/providers/localcontainer` pass.



---

## Update — addressed ClawSweeper review (rebased on the fixed #206)

- **P1 — immutable image identity:** fixed in base #206 (create commits to an immutable digest, then applies a digest-unique tag `crabbox-checkpoint-<name>-<short-digest>`; verify/delete reference the recorded digest; a duplicate-name regression was added). This branch is rebased onto that fix, so the inherited mutable-tag blocker is gone.
- **P2 — docker-socket fork could mask the saved workdir:** `ApplyNativeCheckpointForkConfig` now clears `LocalContainer.DockerSocket` for docker-commit forks, so the host work-root mount can't hide the committed image's workspace. Covered by `TestApplyNativeCheckpointForkConfigDisablesDockerSocket`.

### Proof — create -> dup-name -> verify -> fork -> delete

```
$ go test -tags dockerproof -run TestDockerCommitCheckpointLifecycleProof -v ./internal/cli/
    CREATE:    image=crabbox-checkpoint-proof-fd11fdb40841 id=fd11fdb40841 state=available kind=docker-commit direct=true
    DUP-NAME:  second checkpoint is distinct -> image=crabbox-checkpoint-proof-126889069297 id=126889069297
    VERIFY:    image present (next_action -> fork_or_delete_local), id=fd11fdb40841
    FORK:      booted container from checkpoint image -> forked-from-checkpoint
    DELETE:    Untagged + Deleted sha256:fd11fdb40841...
    CONFIRMED: removed
--- PASS: TestDockerCommitCheckpointLifecycleProof
```

Fork unit tests (image set, Resource preference, kind rejection, docker-socket cleared) plus `go build ./...` and `go vet` all pass. Built/proven in `golang:1.26` + static docker client against host Docker `29.0.1`.
target.author: anagnorisis2peripeteia
